page: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Lsv4 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, configured with 16 physical cores and 32 vCPUs via hyperthreading. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX (Advanced Matrix Extensions). The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes 7679 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across several domains. The server achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 2147.0 and a multi-core score of 18442.0, with notable strength in image processing at 18415.0 and text processing. Memory performance is highlighted by a strong read-only large block bandwidth of 197.62 GB/sec. In data processing tasks, the server demonstrates strong single-threaded gzip compression at 31.48 GB/sec and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression at 209.27 GB/sec. For AI workloads, the hardware-assisted AMX instructions support top-tier text generation speeds of 392.66 tokens/sec on small LLM models, though performance drops to a weak 2.78 tokens/sec for large 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the L32s_v4 offers high local storage density and a balanced memory-to-vCPU ratio without the added cost of dedicated GPU hardware. However, the lack of a GPU limits its utility for large-scale deep learning training, as evidenced by the low text generation speeds on larger LLM models. This server is optimized for storage-intensive and memory-heavy workloads, making it highly suitable for large relational databases, NoSQL databases, data warehousing, and high-throughput static web serving.
description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is a storage-optimized server from the Lsv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor. Operating at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 system provides 32 vCPUs across 16 physical cores, backed by 256.0 GB of RAM and 7679 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability. Benchmark data shows strong capabilities, including a Geekbench multi-core score of 18442.0, a large-block memory read bandwidth of 197.62 GB/sec, and top-tier image processing performance. The CPU supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, enabling efficient small-model LLM inference, though large-model text generation remains a bottleneck due to the absence of a GPU. This server is highly suited for high-throughput databases, data warehousing, and static web serving workloads requiring substantial local NVMe capacity and high memory density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 is an Intel Xeon-powered server with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7679 GB of local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and storage-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L32s_v4 features 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 7679 GB of local storage, ideal for databases and storage-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and extensive local NVMe storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 32 vCPUs and 4.2 GHz clock speed
256.0 GB of system memory paired with 7679 GB of local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Strong performance in large-block memory read bandwidth and image processing
Best suited for large databases, data warehousing, and static web serving
